BLDC Motor Control
  • Core Module
  • src.core.simulation_engine
  • View page source

src.core.simulation_engine

Simulation Engine Module

This module implements the main simulation loop that coordinates motor model, control blocks, load profile, and data logging.

Provides efficient real-time simulation with optimized calculations.

author:

BLDC Control Team

version:

1.0.0

Added in version 1.0.0: Initial implementation of simulation engine

Classes

SimulationEngine(motor, load_profile[, dt, ...])

Main simulation engine for BLDC motor control.
class src.core.simulation_engine.SimulationEngine(motor, load_profile, dt=0.0001, max_history=100000, supply_profile=None, pfc_controller=None, pfc_window_samples=128, hardware_interface=None, current_sense=None, compute_backend='auto')[source]

Bases: object

Main simulation engine for BLDC motor control.

Orchestrates motor simulation with: - Motor model state evolution - Load profile application - Voltage input (from SVM or control blocks) - Data logging and history tracking

Features: - Efficient NumPy-based calculations - Real-time data logging - State history tracking - Simulation time management

Example:
>>> params = MotorParameters(...)
>>> motor = BLDCMotor(params, dt=0.0001)
>>> load = ConstantLoad(torque=1.0)
>>> engine = SimulationEngine(motor, load, dt=0.0001)
>>>
>>> for i in range(10000):
...     voltages = np.array([10, -5, -5])
...     engine.step(voltages)
>>>
>>> history = engine.get_history()
__init__(motor, load_profile, dt=0.0001, max_history=100000, supply_profile=None, pfc_controller=None, pfc_window_samples=128, hardware_interface=None, current_sense=None, compute_backend='auto')[source]

Initialize simulation engine.

Parameters:

  • motor (BLDCMotor) – BLDC motor model (BLDCMotor)

  • load_profile (LoadProfile) – Load profile (LoadProfile)

  • dt (float) – Simulation time step [seconds], defaults to 0.0001

  • max_history (int) – Maximum history samples to store, defaults to 100000

Raises:

ValueError – If dt <= 0

set_inverter_telemetry(telemetry)[source]

Update latest inverter telemetry packet from the modulation stage.

Return type:

None

get_inverter_state()[source]

Return latest inverter telemetry and runtime diagnostics.

Return type:

Dict[str, float | int | bool]

set_pwm_frequency(pwm_frequency_hz)[source]

Configure PWM/control period metadata used for timing telemetry.

Return type:

None

record_control_timing(calc_duration_s, control_period_s=None)[source]

Record controller compute duration and update CPU-load estimates.

Return type:

None

get_control_timing_state()[source]

Return runtime control-timing and CPU-load telemetry.

Return type:

Dict[str, float]

set_compute_backend(backend)[source]

Set compute backend policy: auto, cpu, or gpu.

Return type:

None

get_compute_backend_state()[source]

Return currently selected compute backend state.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

step(voltages, log_data=True)[source]

Execute one simulation step.

Updates motor state, applies load, and optionally logs data.

Parameters:

  • voltages (ndarray) – 3-phase input voltages [v_a, v_b, v_c] [V]

  • log_data (bool) – Whether to log data to history, defaults to True

Return type:

None

Warning

Set log_data=False during high-frequency real-time control loops, then call log_data() periodically to reduce overhead.

configure_power_factor_control(enabled, target_pf=0.95, kp=0.1, ki=1.0, max_compensation_var=10000.0, window_samples=128)[source]

Enable/disable closed-loop PFC telemetry hook.

Return type:

None

get_power_factor_control_state()[source]

Return current PFC telemetry and controller state.

Return type:

Dict[str, float | bool]

get_efficiency_state()[source]

Return current efficiency and loss telemetry.

Return type:

Dict[str, float]

configure_hardware_interface(enabled)[source]

Enable or disable hardware I/O execution path at runtime.

Return type:

None

get_hardware_state()[source]

Return hardware backend execution status and diagnostics.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

log_data(load_torque=None)[source]

Manual data logging (for external control loops).

Call this periodically when using step() with log_data=False.

Parameters:

load_torque (Optional[float]) – Load torque to log. If None, uses current load profile value.

Return type:

None

reset(initial_speed=0.0)[source]

Reset simulation to initial state.

Clears all history and resets motor and load profile.

Parameters:

initial_speed (float) – Initial motor speed [rad/s], defaults to 0.0

Return type:

None

get_history()[source]

Get all simulation history as numpy arrays.

Returns:

Dictionary with history data arrays

Return type:

Dict[str, ndarray]

Keys:

  • ‘time’: Simulation time points [s]

  • ‘currents_a’: Phase A currents [A]

  • ‘currents_b’: Phase B currents [A]

  • ‘currents_c’: Phase C currents [A]

  • ‘omega’: Angular velocity [rad/s]

  • ‘theta’: Angular position [rad]

  • ‘speed’: Speed [RPM]

  • ‘emf_a’: Back-EMF phase A [V]

  • ‘emf_b’: Back-EMF phase B [V]

  • ‘emf_c’: Back-EMF phase C [V]

  • ‘torque’: Electromagnetic torque [N*m]

  • ‘load_torque’: Load torque [N*m]

  • ‘voltages_a’: Applied voltage phase A [V]

  • ‘voltages_b’: Applied voltage phase B [V]

  • ‘voltages_c’: Applied voltage phase C [V]

get_current_state()[source]

Get current motor state.

Returns:

Current motor state variables

Return type:

Dict[str, float]

get_controller_phase_currents()[source]

Return phase currents as seen by the control algorithm.

Return type:

ndarray

get_simulation_info()[source]

Get simulation metadata.

Returns:

Simulation information

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

Keys:

  • ‘time’: Current simulation time [s]

  • ‘step_count’: Number of steps executed

  • ‘dt’: Time step [s]

  • ‘history_size’: Number of logged data points
